title: Isolation of Viral Like Particles (VLP) from Tissues of Molluscs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"tks2","text":" This is a protocol especielly for the isolation of Viral Like Particles (VLP) from the tissues of Molluscs, such as abalone, oyster, clam,  scallop and so on. This maybe also applicable for other animal tissues, such as shrimp and fish, based on the principle of the protocol. But we havn't verified this idea yet.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2kp7k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4sve1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a5fmd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f77nn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ftk92","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9s3si","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d4jqk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Tissue samples are sliced off and freezed in liquid Nitrogen instantly. Then transfer to -80℃ freezer after return to laboratory. Visceral mass and gills are the best target tissues for isolation, as viruses are easily concentrated in those organs. We have tested with from minimum 20 mg of oyster spat samples to maximum 20 g of abalone samples for VLP isolation. And we all acquired certain amount of Viral reads after seqenced by Illunima platform.

Add about 1 to 1.5 times volume of SB (Stabilizing Buffer, 0.2 M NaCl, 50 mM Tris-HCl, 5 mM CaCl 2 , and 5 mM MgCl 2 , pH 7.5) and grind with grinding machine at 4℃. For example, adding 1-1.5 mL SB buffer to homogenize 1g tissues Undetermined
Add extra SB to fix a final volume at 3-5 times of weightened tissue, and make it fully homogenized.
Add extra SB to fix a final volume at 5-10 times of weightened tissue, if you want to maximize the dissolution of VLP from cell debris.

Supernatants then filtrated with Millex-HV 0.45 μm and 0.22 μm filters (Merck Millipore, Billerica, MA, USA) sequentially 0.22 μm filters will effectively remove most of bacterial cells. Please NOTICE that 0.22 μm not suitable for enriching viruses larger than 200 nm in diameter.
Sucrose cushion (sucrose-SB solution, 28% w/w) was added to the bottom of the ultracentrifuge tube first.

Conduct differential centrifugation at 4 °C, sequentially from 1,000 3,000 5,000 8,000 10,000 g, each time for 5 min. Cell debris will precipitate to the bottom of tubes, and VLP will be keeped in supernatant. 00:25:00, Centrifuging
Centrifuging at 300,000 g for 2 h, then remove supernatant. VLPs are just in the precipitation You can pause at this step, and put VLP pellet into -80℃ for reservation. 02:00:00, Ultracentrifuging
Put above homogenate into three freezing and thawing cycles Use liquid nitrogen and 50℃ water bath to proceed the cycle. But it needs to be removed immediately after melting in the water bath.
Add the appropriate amount of DNase solution （820 μl ddH 2 O, 90 μl 10× DNase I Buffer, 90 μl DNase I (1 U/μl)） to the tube, and use pipette tip to fully suspend precipitation.
Transfer supernatants (step 7) into ultracentrifuge tubes, softly laying onto 28% (w/w) sucrose cushion. Operation with carefulness, as 28% sucrose solution is easily disturbed
Incubating at 37 °C for 60 min in shaker at 150 rpm. 01:00:00, DNase Incubating
Centrifuging at 10000 g for 5 min and then transferr supernatant to a new tube. 00:05:00, Centrifuging
creator: Jing-Zhe (Ginger) Jiang
